title: Information Theory — SuperBEST v4 Cost Analysis

operator_costs._note: v5 (ADD-T1): add=2n for ALL real x,y. add_pos and add_gen distinction eliminated.

key_insight: Information theory is built on p * ln(p) — ln costs 1n, mul costs 2n. In v5, add=2n for all real inputs (ADD-T1), so the sign-definiteness question no longer drives costs. Shannon and KL divergence now have nearly identical per-term costs (Shannon: 8N-3 due to neg overhead; KL: 7N-2). The add_gen bottleneck is eliminated.

sign_analysis.verdict_v4: Restructure as sum of -p_i*ln(p_i): each term non-negative => add_pos=3n
sign_analysis.verdict_v5: add=2n for all (ADD-T1); add_pos=3n is subsumed. Same neg-first strategy, but additions now cost 2n instead of 3n.

breakdown.strategy: Compute neg(ln(p_i)) first, then mul, yielding positive terms. Sum with add (2n in v5).
breakdown.additions_v5: (N-1) * add = 2(N-1)  [add=2n, ADD-T1; terms are non-negative but add=2n is universal now]

rejected_strategy.description: Compute p_i*ln(p_i) directly (negative terms), sum with add, then negate
rejected_strategy.note_v5: v5: both strategies now differ only by the outer neg(2n); direct sum 5N vs neg-first 7N-2. For large N, DIRECT SUM (5N) is actually cheaper! The neg-first strategy is no longer optimal in v5.
rejected_strategy.v5_optimal_strategy: Direct: mul(p_i, ln_p_i) for each i (3n per term = 3N), sum with add (2*(N-1)), then neg(2n). Total: 3N + 2N-2+2 = 5N. Simpler and cheaper than neg-first (7N-2) for N >= 3.

equations.name: KL Divergence
equations.domain: Information theory, Bayesian inference, variational methods

sign_analysis.single_term_sign: p_i*ln(p_i/q_i) can be positive (p_i > q_i) or negative (p_i < q_i)
sign_analysis.global_sum_sign: D_KL >= 0 (Gibbs inequality), but individual terms are mixed sign
sign_analysis.verdict_v4: Could not use add_pos — required add_gen=11n for all N-1 additions
sign_analysis.verdict_v5: add=2n for all real (ADD-T1); sign ambiguity is no longer a cost factor. Same 2n per addition regardless of sign.

equations.cost_vs_shannon: v5: KL = 7N-2 vs Shannon = 8N-3. KL is now CHEAPER per-term (no neg node needed; div+ln+mul=5n vs ln+neg+mul=5n — wait: KL has div(2n) extra but no neg(2n), Shannon has neg(2n) but no div; net: both 5n per term). Additions: both 2n. Shannon has extra outer neg-first restructuring. The 2x cost ratio from v4 collapses entirely.

equations.name: Cross-Entropy Loss
equations.domain: ML training loss, information theory, maximum likelihood

sign_analysis.verdict: Each term p_i * (-ln q_i) >= 0 — same structure as Shannon entropy => add_pos

equations.structural_isomorphism: H(P,Q) and H(P,P)=H(P) have identical EML cost (8N-3). The ML cross-entropy loss is the same formula as Shannon entropy with the predicted distribution substituted for the true distribution. Zero cost difference.

equations.name: Mutual Information
equations.domain: Information theory, feature selection, causal inference
equations.n_dependence: O(|X|*|Y|), double sum over joint distribution

sign_analysis.single_term_sign: p(x,y)*ln(p(x,y)/(p(x)p(y))) can be positive or negative individually
sign_analysis.global_sum_sign: I(X;Y) >= 0, but individual cells are mixed sign
sign_analysis.verdict_v5: add=2n for all real (ADD-T1); mixed-sign cells no longer penalized

equations.name: Perplexity
equations.n_dependence: O(N), N = sequence length or vocabulary

sign_analysis.verdict_v4: add_gen=11n required since values are negative (add_pos requires positivity)
sign_analysis.verdict_v5: add=2n for all real (ADD-T1); the negative-value penalty is eliminated

steps.note: v5: add=2n regardless of sign; v4: add_gen=11n
negate the negative sum to get positive

equations.cost_insight: v5: Perplexity costs 3N+3 vs Shannon's 8N-3. The add_gen penalty is gone. The remaining overhead (neg+div+exp = 5n fixed) is now small relative to N. v4 overhead was 4N-3 growing with N due to add_gen dominance; v5 overhead is just 5 fixed nodes.

equations.name: Entropy Rate (Markov Chain)
equations.domain: Information theory, stochastic processes, ergodic theory

sign_analysis.inner_H_i: Shannon entropy of row i — each inner term is non-negative

inner_loop.description: For each state i, compute H_i = Shannon entropy of transition row P_ij

outer_sum.note: v5: add=2n (ADD-T1); mu_i*H_i >= 0, but add_pos=3n is subsumed by universal add=2n

equations.name: Conditional Entropy
equations.domain: Information theory, channel capacity, decision trees

sign_analysis.verdict: All |X||Y| terms are non-negative => add_pos for full sum

breakdown.additions_v5: (|X||Y|-1) * add = 2(|X||Y|-1)  [add=2n, ADD-T1; terms are non-negative but add is universal]

equations.comparison_with_mi: v5: Both conditional entropy (9|X||Y|-2) and mutual information (9|X||Y|-2) now cost the SAME! The entire gap from v4 (10|X||Y|-3 vs 18|X||Y|-11) was driven by add_pos vs add_gen. With add=2n for all, the sign advantage of H(Y|X) is eliminated — both converge to 9|X||Y|-2.

equations.name: Jensen-Shannon Divergence
equations.domain: Information theory, distributional distance, generative models

step_1_mixture.description: Compute M_i = (p_i + q_i)/2 for each i

ops.note: v5: add=2n for all (ADD-T1); p_i, q_i >= 0, was add_pos=3n

step_2_H_M.description: Shannon entropy of mixture M (v5 cost)

step_5_average_entropy.note: v5: add=2n (ADD-T1); both entropies >= 0, was add_pos=3n

step_6_subtract.note: sub=2n always; result JSD >= 0 by convexity of entropy

equations.cost_insight: v5: JSD = 3 Shannon evaluations (7N-2 each) + mixture (4N) + scalar ops (6n) = 25N. v4 was 29N-2. The savings come from: (a) mixture step: add_pos 3n→add 2n saves 1n per N term = N saved; (b) each Shannon eval: 8N-3 → 7N-2 saves N-1 per eval = 3*(N-1) = 3N-3 saved; total ~4N saved consistent with 29N-2 → 25N.

isomorphisms.name: Shannon = Boltzmann entropy
isomorphisms.note: Identical formula; k_B just scales the result by a free constant. Zero EML cost difference.

isomorphisms.name: KL divergence = free energy difference
isomorphisms.note: v5: KL divergence now equals Shannon entropy cost (7N-2). The 2x cost ratio from v4 is eliminated by ADD-T1.

isomorphisms.name: Cross-entropy loss = Shannon cross-entropy
isomorphisms.note: Identical formula; ML training minimizes Shannon cross-entropy. Cost is the same whether viewed as ML or IT.

isomorphisms.name: Perplexity = partition function analog
isomorphisms.IT_form: PP = exp(-1/N * sum ln p_i) = exp(H)  [for uniform]
isomorphisms.note: Both are exponentials of sums of log-probabilities/energies. PP measures average surprise; Z measures accessible states.

isomorphisms.name: Mutual information = channel capacity core
isomorphisms.note: I(X;Y) is the per-distribution cost (18|X||Y|-11); optimization over input distribution adds additional structure.

summary_table.columns_note: v5 costs shown (SuperBEST v5, add=2n for all real x,y, ADD-T1)
rows: v5: add=2n saves 1n per addition vs add_pos=3n; optimal strategy shifts to direct sum (5N) for large N
v5: add=2n saves 9n per addition; KL now equals Shannon cost per term
v5: add=2n saves 9n per cell addition
v5: add=2n for negative ln values; dramatic reduction
quadratic: inner Shannon loop per state (7N-2 per row in v5)
v5: add_pos 3n→add 2n saves 1n per cell addition
v5: 3 Shannon evals (7N-2 each) + mixture (4N) + scalars; saves ~4N vs v4
summary_table.note_N10: For equations 4, 6, 7: N=10 means |X|=|Y|=10 (100 cells/pairs) or |S|=10 states

eml_domain_assessment.verdict: Information theory is the most EML-native applied domain after pure mathematics
rationale: Core primitive is p*ln(p): ln=1n + mul=2n = 3n per weighted log term (unchanged in v5)
Shannon entropy v5: 7N-2 (v4: 8N-3). KL divergence v5: 7N-2 (v4: 16N-11). They are now EQUAL.
Cross-entropy ML loss is structurally identical to IT entropy: zero overhead for ML training
v5 key insight: sign-definiteness NO LONGER drives cost. add=2n for all real inputs (ADD-T1).
All N-term sums collapse to (alpha_0 + 2)*N - 2 regardless of sign structure
The KL vs Shannon 2x cost ratio from v4 is ELIMINATED. The positive/signed split is obsolete.
eml_domain_assessment.design_principle: v5: The central optimization question shifts from sign-definiteness to structural complexity. The add_gen vs add_pos distinction is gone. Focus on minimizing mul/div chains and per-term node count. For Shannon, the direct-sum strategy (5N, computing p*ln(p) then negating sum) is now optimal for N>=3.
eml_domain_assessment.v4_design_principle_obsolete: v4: The central optimization question was: are summands sign-definite? If yes, 3n/addition; if no, 11n/addition. This 8n gap no longer exists in v5.
